prompt:
I run the front desk rota at our aquatic centre, and render_week.sh has printed the weekly lane sheet for two summers straight. This week it just stopped. It still announces itself and then dies without printing a single line of the sheet:

    $ bash render_week.sh board.tmpl 'July 20'
    rendering week of July 20
    render_week.sh: line 25: unexpected EOF while looking for matching `''

Nothing is missing at the end of the file -- I opened it and the last line is the same echo it has always been. And line 25 looks fine to me; the quotes pair up when I read them out loud. bash -n says the same thing, so it never even gets to sed.

The sheet wording itself was correct before this and none of it is supposed to change -- same facility line, same desk extension, every placeholder filled everywhere it appears. Fix the script so it renders exactly what it used to. bash test_poolsheet.sh is the acceptance gate; leave the test file alone.
